name: Ojos-Albos and Cruz de Hierro Loop
name_original: La Atalaya, pinturas rupestres y Mediana de Voltoya — circular desde Ojos-Albos
description: Explore the moderate 17.2-mile La Atalaya mountain biking route from Ojos-Albos, featuring ancient rock art and scenic views.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Ojos-Albos offers a diverse landscape for riders. The region features rolling hills and open fields, with routes often traversing rural areas and connecting small villages. Significant elevation changes are present on many trails, providing varied challenges and rewarding views. The terrain includes a mix of dirt roads and paths, suitable for exploring the local countryside. Best mountain bike trails around Ojos-Albos The most popular mountain bike trail is Ojos-Albos and Cruz de Hierro Loop, a 17.2 miles (27.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 50 minutes to complete. This route offers a comprehensive loop through the local landscape, including the notable Cruz de Hierro. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Views of Ávila – View of the Valley and Fields loop from Guimorcondo, a moderate 24.7 miles (39.8 km) path. This trail provides expansive views of Ávila, the valley, and surrounding fields. Local mountain bikers also love the Ermita de San Antonio – El Cubillo Hermitage loop from Aldeavieja, a 28.7 miles (46.2 km) trail leading through rural landscapes and past historic hermitages, often completed in about 3 hours 55 minutes. Mountain biking around Ojos-Albos is defined by open fields, scenic valleys, and paths connecting historic sites. The network offers a range of options, from easy to moderate routes, catering to different skill levels. The routes in Ojos-Albos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a limited number of reviews. More than 30 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Ojos-Albos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Ojos-Albos?
A: There are 14 mountain bike trails available around Ojos-Albos, offering a diverse range of routes for exploration.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain bike trails in Ojos-Albos?
A: The mountain bike trails in Ojos-Albos primarily range from easy to moderate difficulty. There are 2 easy trails and 12 moderate trails, making the area accessible for various skill levels.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Ojos-Albos?
A: Yes, Ojos-Albos offers 2 easy mountain bike trails, perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes typically feature less challenging terrain and elevation changes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Ojos-Albos?
A: The terrain around Ojos-Albos features a mix of rolling hills, open fields, and rural areas. Trails often traverse dirt roads and paths, connecting small villages and offering significant elevation changes for varied challenges and rewarding views.

Q: Are there any loop mountain bike trails in Ojos-Albos?
A: Yes, many trails in Ojos-Albos are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1092688221/ojos-albos-and-cruz-de-hierro-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos-Albos and Cruz de Hierro Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option that provides a comprehensive tour of the local landscape.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see while mountain biking in Ojos-Albos?
A: While mountain biking, you can encounter several interesting points. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4846071" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo Azálvaro Bike Path&lt;/a&gt; is a dedicated cycling route, and you might pass through the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2458937" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Lancha&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4432815" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañada Real Soriana Occidental&lt;/a&gt;, an ancient drovers' road, also offers a unique historical element to some routes.

Q: Are there any trails that offer scenic views of the surrounding area?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38249650" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Ávila – View of the Valley and Fields loop from Guimorcondo&lt;/a&gt; is specifically known for its expansive vistas of Ávila, the valley, and the surrounding fields, providing a rewarding visual experience.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Ojos-Albos?
A: The routes in Ojos-Albos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Riders often praise the diverse landscape, the mix of dirt roads and paths, and the rewarding views from the significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by water features or reservoirs?
A: Yes, some routes in the broader area may bring you close to water features. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6162407" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navalperal de Pinares Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a notable highlight in the vicinity that could be incorporated into a longer ride or visited separately.

Q: What is the typical duration for a mountain bike ride in Ojos-Albos?
A: Ride durations vary depending on the trail. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38156863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Antonio – El Cubillo Hermitage loop from Aldeavieja&lt;/a&gt; typically takes about 3 hours 55 minutes, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1092688221/ojos-albos-and-cruz-de-hierro-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ojos-Albos and Cruz de Hierro Loop&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in around 2 hours 50 minutes.

Q: Are there any trails that connect historic sites or hermitages?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38156863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Antonio – El Cubillo Hermitage loop from Aldeavieja&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, leading through rural landscapes and past historic hermitages, offering a cultural element to your ride.

Q: What is the longest mountain bike trail available in Ojos-Albos?
A: The longest trail listed is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38156863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de San Antonio – El Cubillo Hermitage loop from Aldeavieja&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 46.2 kilometers (28.7 miles).
tips: Rolling near Ojos-Albos is this beautiful mountain range altered by technological progress. A wide dirt road without technical difficulty is accompanied by the windmills of the Aldeavieja Wind Farm.

Mingubela Cave, also known as Peña Mingubela, is a fascinating rock shelter located near the small village of Ojos Albos in Ávila, Spain. This site is known for its ancient cave paintings, which depict various figures, including warriors, some of whom are shown holding shields or in prayer positions. The cave is divided by a stone column, which adds to its unique structure.

If you are interested in visiting, it is important to note that the cave is only accessible on foot and the path can be quite steep, so it is advisable to bring a flashlight and seek precise directions from locals. Additionally, there are several hiking trails in the area, such as the circular trail from Ojos Albos that includes La Atalaya and Mingubela Cave.
Located between Ávila and Segovia, Mediana de Voltoya is surrounded by nature and offers a quiet environment ideal for having a drink in one of its bars.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route is circular and starts and ends in Ojos-Albos, making it convenient to park there. Look for suitable parking areas within the village.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on trails in the Sierra de Ávila, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially when passing through populated areas, near livestock, or in areas with wildlife. Be sure to carry water for your dog.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best seasons for mountain biking in the Ávila region, offering pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be very hot, and winter might bring cold weather or snow at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are typically required for mountain biking on this route in the Sierra de Ojos Albos. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Rated as 'moderate' with a significant elevation gain of around 708 meters over 27.6 kilometers, this route is better suited for riders with some mountain biking experience. While e-bikes can make it more accessible, it might be challenging for absolute beginners or very young families.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: As a mountain bike (BTT) route, you can expect a mix of off-road sections, including dirt tracks and possibly some technical parts. The region's landscape often features hilly or mountainous terrain typical of Ávila province.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several notable points of interest. You'll pass by the Aldeavieja Wind Farm, the ancient Cueva Mingubela Rock Art, and the Church of Mediana de Voltoya. The name also suggests views of 'La Atalaya' (The Watchtower) and the 'Pinturas Rupestres' (Cave Paintings).

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this 27.6-kilometer route is approximately 2 hours and 50 minutes (10,201 seconds) for an average mountain biker. Your actual time may vary depending on your pace, stops, and fitness level.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several official trails, including the Camino Natural de Campo Azálvaro (4%), Carril bici de la Cañada Real (4%), Ruta del Sureste (4%), Cañada Real Soriana Occidental (4% and 1%), and Cañada de los Margatos (2%).

faqs.question: What should I bring or wear for this mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and off-road terrain, wear appropriate cycling attire, a helmet, and sturdy mountain biking shoes. Carry plenty of water, snacks, a basic repair kit, and navigation tools. Sunscreen and layers are advisable depending on the season.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through Ojos-Albos and Mediana de Voltoya, where you may find local establishments like cafes or restaurants to refuel. It's always a good idea to check opening hours in advance, especially in smaller villages.
